calendar.matters:
DEMURRER to Amended PETITION FOR WRIT OF MANDATE/ PROHIBITION/ CERTIFICATION
Off Calendar Nov-15-2023 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: APPEARANCES:

John H. George; Deputy City Attorney; 415-554-4223; appearing as counsel for Respondents City and County of San Francisco and Office of the Treasurer & Tax Colector.
B. Douglas Robbins; Tong Robbins LLP; 650-240-2404; appearing as counsel for Petitioners Trinity Management Services, et al.

Respondents contest the court's tentative ruling and argues that Petitioner should not be granted leave to amend. Petitioners submit to the court's tentative ruling. After reviewing the pleadings and listening to oral arguments the court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

RESPONDENTS' DEMURRER TO AMENDED PETITION FOR WRIT OF MANDATE is SUSTAINED with leave to amend to address in good faith all issues raised in the demurrer. Respondent's request for judicial notice is GRANTED as to the San Francisco parking tax returns, San Francisco Ordinance 206-13, letters to Petitioners' counsel denying refund claims, and S.F. Business & Tax Regulations Code section 6.2-21; 6.7-1; 6.9-1; 6.11-1 through 6.15-3; 602; and 604. (Evid. Code, section 452(d).)

Prevailing party to prepare order in compliance with CRC 3.1312(b). Judge: Charles F. Haines; Clerk: Kenneth B. Hunt; Brianna Rudd, CSR #13668, briannarudd@gmail.com, 209-747-1306 (D501)
